subject: Continuous Random Variables
question: Suppose that $X$ has a uniform distribution over the interval $(0, 1)$. Find (a) $F(x)$. (b) Show that $\Pr(a \le X \le a + b)$ for $a, b \ge 0$, $a + b \le 1$ depends only on $b$.
answer: (a) $F(x) = \begin{cases} 0 & x < 0 \\ x & 0 \le x \le 1 \\ 1 & x > 1 \end{cases}$ (b) $\Pr(a \le X \le a + b) = F(a + b) - F(a) = a + b - a = b$
explanation: Answer: (a) $F(x) = \begin{cases} 0 & x < 0 \\ x & 0 \le x \le 1 \\ 1 & x > 1 \end{cases}$ (b) $\Pr(a \le X \le a + b) = F(a + b) - F(a) = a + b - a = b$
